user_query:
I need a reproducible, skeptical-angle briefing from the City Wildlife–Community Budget Roundtable, using the provided minutes and attendee list. Please do all of the following and keep everything within the local workspace:

Inputs (read-only):
- input/meeting_minutes_session1.md
- input/meeting_minutes_session2.md
- input/attendees.csv

Deliverables (write these exact paths):
1) out/metrics_by_program.csv — Combine the “Metrics discussed” tables from both minutes files by ProgramID and compute per-program aggregates. Columns (in this order):
   - ProgramID
   - ProgramName
   - TotalBudgetUSD (sum across sessions)
   - TotalHouseholdsServed (sum across sessions)
   - TotalHumanIncidentsPrevented (sum across sessions)
   - TotalWildlifeSaved (sum across sessions)
   - CostPerHouseholdUSD = TotalBudgetUSD / TotalHouseholdsServed (if denominator is 0, write NA)
   - CostPerIncidentUSD = TotalBudgetUSD / TotalHumanIncidentsPrevented (if denominator is 0, write NA)
   Sort rows by TotalBudgetUSD descending.

2) out/attendance_summary.csv — Summarize attendees by AffiliationType from input/attendees.csv with columns: AffiliationType, Count, PercentOfTotal. PercentOfTotal should reflect each group’s share of all rows. Sort by Count descending.

3) out/meeting_summary.md — A concise summary for my notes that includes:
   - Overall totals: combined TotalBudgetUSD, TotalHouseholdsServed, TotalHumanIncidentsPrevented, and TotalWildlifeSaved across all programs.
   - The top 2 programs by TotalBudgetUSD (name and amount) and one sentence assessing budget efficiency from a human-needs perspective.
   - A bulleted list of all unique action items extracted from the “Action Items” sections in both meeting minutes (deduplicated, and include the ProgramID when it appears in the bullet text).
   - A short callout listing any programs with CostPerIncidentUSD > 3000.

4) out/email_to_editor.txt — Draft an email to my editor laying out a skeptical angle on budget efficiency vs. human impact, 120–200 words. Include at least two computed stats from your outputs (e.g., the overall total budget and one high cost-per-incident figure), propose a framing that questions whether conservation funds might be better directed to human services if outcomes are weak, and end with a clear ask for approval on next steps. Start the file with a Subject: line, then a blank line, then the body.

Reproducibility requirement (make command-line use essential):
- Implement a small command-line script (e.g., summarize_roundtable.py or a shell pipeline) that reads the inputs and generates the data outputs in out/. Run it locally to produce all artifacts. Save:
  - out/run.log capturing the script’s stdout from a single end-to-end run.
  - out/REPRODUCE.md with the exact shell command(s) you executed (include interpreter invocation and arguments, e.g., python3 summarize_roundtable.py --minutes input/meeting_minutes_session1.md input/meeting_minutes_session2.md --attendees input/attendees.csv --outdir out).

Assumptions and constraints:
- Parse the Markdown tables under the section heading “Metrics discussed” in each minutes file. Sum metrics across sessions by ProgramID.
- Use integers as provided for sums; derived cost metrics may be fractional. If any denominator is zero, write NA for that derived field.
- Do not invent data; all numbers in deliverables must trace back to the provided inputs and your computations.
- All paths are relative to the workspace; do not fetch anything from the Internet.

I care most about accuracy of the aggregates, the clarity of the skeptical summary, and a professional email draft that cites the computed numbers. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
